name: Puyehue-Cordón Caulle
eruptionHistory: The 2011-2012 eruption produced massive ash clouds that disrupted aviation across the southern hemisphere and deposited heavy ashfall on Argentine Patagonia.
description: The 2011 eruption of Puyehue-Cordón Caulle was one of the most significant Chilean eruptions in decades, producing rhyolite lava flows and ash that affected Argentina and beyond.
